description: Unified open-items register — ADC-SYS-001. Every open engineering item across all product documents, with cross-product gating made explicit. CDU items carry ADC-assigned IDs (CDU-G*) because the source BOM lists its release gates unnumbered.

items.title: Confirm OCP 102-inch metric typo (2210 vs 2590.91 mm) before metric drawings
items.status: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 — typo confirmed against the OCP source PDF (Table 18.2.1 prints 2210mm; Figure 18.3.1 prints 2590.91; 102 in = 2,590.8 mm exact, and 2,210 mm = 87 in would be narrower than the 90-in post centers it encloses). Inch values govern; ADC metric drawings print 2,590.8 mm. Proof: ops/research/2026-06-11-hac-oi-02-ocp-102in-metric-typo.md

Confirm 630T5 Global catalog numbers with the Starline/Legrand rep. Source: ops/research/2026-06-11-starline-t5-busway-weights.md

items.title: Name the AI-native operating system (resolves COMM-001 for the platform layer)

items.title: ADC-NSTAR-001 Rev 0.2 (governing N-star block definition, products/NStar/) issued 2026-06-11 — awaiting Scott approval; codifies decisions 0004+0005: block = 5 MW IT (4x reference cluster = 4,992 kW) + one 10 MW MGN-BOD-002 node
gates: all N-star capacity claims in proposals

items.title: N-star block electrical seam: node 4.16 kV bus to the 16 cluster RPPs (transformation, switchgear, redundancy topology satisfying the ADC-CLU-BOM-001 §3 upstream rule) — the block's one missing electrical drawing; first instance = ADC-TRAP-ELEC-001
items.status: ADC-TRAP-ELEC-001 Rev 0.1 ISSUED 2026-06-11 — pending Scott approval. Topology: 16 dedicated 4.16kV->415Y/240V transformers per block (1:1 with RPPs, [L] per the upstream rule), fed from a block distribution switchgear lineup off the node bus. Sizing/switchgear/siting open at ELE-OI-05/06/07.

items.title: N-star pairing-ratio confirmation: derive block PUE from reference-bay (TCS/HAC) cooling overheads + W32/W40 heat-rejection auxiliary power (asserted band 1.15-1.25); reconcile 78 kW avg rack basis vs NVL72 actuals
items.status: ADC-NSTAR-001 Rev 0.2 ISSUED 2026-06-11 (pending Scott approval) -- mechanical+CDU PUE derived at ~1.10-1.14 [W] from colo/Heat_Rejection_5MW_IT.pdf (W32/W40 evaporative + chiller-trim hybrid) and ADC-CDU-DES-BOM-001 4x74kW CDU draw; 1.15-1.25 band retained, decomposed (mechanical+CDU confirmed, ~0.01-0.11 house/aux+de-rate residual remains [O]). Rack-density reconciliation split out to SYS-OI-11. NOTE 2026-06-11 evening: FWS-COOL-001 Rev 0.1 drop (products/FWS/) adopts the OPPOSITE architecture (zero-water dry coolers + always-on chillers, mech ~28% peak / ~15% annualized) -- the 1.10-1.14 derivation only stands if decision 0006 rules for evaporative + trim; see SYS-OI-12.

items.title: Rack-density reconciliation: 78 kW/rack reference-cluster basis (locked 2 MW/500 GPM CDU hydraulic capacity, ADC-CLU-BOM-001 Sec2 / ADC-MOD-TCS-001 Sec3.2) vs full-density NVL72 (120-140 kW/rack, ~50 GPM/rack per ADC-MOD-TCS-001 Sec4.4.1) -- locked CDU supports ~10 NVL72-density racks, not 16; does not change decision 0005 (block IT capacity 4,992 kW unaffected)
gates: any proposal stating an NVL72 rack count per reference cluster

items.title: Seam transformer catalog confirmation: 4.16 kV -> 415Y/240 V, 750 kVA class, 16/block (1:1 with RPPs) -- stock export/OCP unit vs 480Y unit + buck-boost
4.16 kV feeder/breaker AIC ratings for the seam's 16 (or 17, incl. tie) new switchgear positions -- depends on ELEC-002 node fault-level study

Seam distribution-switchgear siting (integral to node switchgear vs separate block distribution lineup) and 4-pad-per-cluster transformer layout -- depends on TRAP-CIVIL-001 hall layout (tracked package-side as TRAP-OI-06)

Heat-rejection architecture decision (0006): evaporative + chiller trim (NSTAR Rev 0.2 PUE basis, colo/Heat_Rejection_5MW_IT.pdf + ADC-FAC-HRS-BOM-001) vs zero-water dry-cooler + chiller plant (FWS-COOL-001 Rev 0.1) -- conflicting architectures for the same 5 MW IT / 10 MW node design point; FWS-COOL-001 also assumes 3x CDU-2500 vs the locked 4x 2 MW Deschutes CDU basis (must re-cut either way)
items.status: decision 0006 DRAFTED 2026-06-11 (ops/decisions/0006-heat-rejection-architecture.md) with recommendation: evaporative + trim stays the N-star standard, dry-cooler plant = water-constrained-site alternate -- awaiting Scott. FWS-RFQ-M-001 Rev 0.2 HELD from vendors meanwhile

items.title: BESS coupling decision (0007): MGN-BOD-002 Rev 0.2 locks [L] AC-coupled 4.16 kV grid-forming PCS (forms bus, black-starts node) vs ESS-BOD-001 Rev 0.2 DC-coupled 800 V-class classification (W-08, sect2) -- 800 V DC path is forward-looking, key equipment not yet generally available (Scott 2026-06-11); decision 0005 already dropped the 800 VDC cassette platform
items.status: decision 0007 DRAFTED 2026-06-11 (ops/decisions/0007-bess-coupling.md) with recommendation: keep locked AC-coupled basis, ESS-BOD re-states coupling at Rev 0.3, 800 V DC tracked as watch item -- awaiting Scott. ESS-RFQ-001 Rev 0.1 HELD from bidders meanwhile

items.title: Chiller/fluid-cooler vendor selection runs at design conditions (FWS-COOL-001 M-1: Trane TOPSS at 26/36C evap, 54C PG30 condenser; or evaporative selections at 0.4% WB if decision 0006 = evaporative) + PHE vendor (M-3, leverage existing ADC Tranter/Alfa Laval quotes)
Adiabatic pre-cool option on dry coolers (FWS-COOL-001 M-2/D-6, open): breaks strict zero-water but cuts ~15-20% array size -- only material if decision 0006 = dry-cooler architecture
FWS electrical coordination (FWS-COOL-001 E-1/E-2): chiller voltage MV vs 480 V against the node single-line; motor-start coordination study against the 6 MW / 3 MWh BESS step envelope

FWS telemetry mapping (FWS-COOL-001 C-1/C-2): chiller/dry-cooler/PHE points into the MGN-TEL-001 tag schema (Thermal domain, D4); HT supply reset sequence (reset ceiling tied to CDU approach) -- the highest-value control sequence
PCS fault-domain split 2x3 MW vs 3x2 MW (ESS-BOD-001 E-1) -- drives HMA single-unit consequence, yard area, one-line; bidders propose split with rationale per ESS-RFQ-001 sect3.1

Vendor LSFT / installation-level non-propagation status (ESS-BOD-001 E-3, gate G-2/G-2a): report in hand is a BID requirement (PS-2, labs booking 3-6 months out); absent installation-level result, SEP-1 propagation-assumed spacing governs yard area

AHJ engagement (ESS-BOD-001 F-1/F-2): confirm adopted IFC/NFPA editions; brief NFPA 855 (2026) baseline + managed burn-down ERP; NFPA 69 CCR vs NFPA 68 venting allocation per container (HMA output + AHJ position; A.6 input weights toward NFPA 69 active ventilation primary)

Liquid-cooled module declaration (ESS-BOD-001 P-1): if Package 1 vendor uses sealed liquid-cooled modules, coolant leak detection enters mandatory scope; ESS-BOD T-2 (BMS protocol DNP3 vs IEC 61850) = the existing RELAY-PROTO decision, cross-linked not duplicated
